semantic.role: CI/CD workflow configuration for automated documentation deployment
responsibilities: Trigger documentation build and deployment on push to main branch
Define build steps using Node.js and npm
Define deployment steps to GitHub Pages
Manage concurrency to prevent multiple concurrent deployments

English.purpose: This document defines a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically builds and deploys the ArchSpine project documentation to GitHub Pages whenever changes are pushed to the main branch affecting docs/ or package files.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for maintainers and contributors who need to understand how documentation is published. It describes the automated pipeline, not the documentation itself.
key_takeaways: Documentation is built using npm ci and a custom 'docs:build' script (likely VitePress).
Deployment only occurs on pushes to the main branch, limited to docs/ or package manifest changes.
Concurrency is configured to cancel in-progress deployments when a new push arrives.
The workflow uses GitHub Pages deployment actions, with artifact upload and deploy steps.
